reasoning: Q6 iteration 1 regressed (29.3→31.14ms) likely due to complexity of combined zone map + SIMD implementation. With 1 iteration remaining, pivot to simpler, isolated optimizations: predicate reordering (selectivity-based) and morsel size tuning. Both are low-risk code changes with 10-20% combined potential gain, avoiding the architectural overhead that caused iteration 1 regression.
focus_areas: Predicate reordering by selectivity: discount (2%) → shipdate (27%) → quantity (50%)
Morsel size tuning: test 75K-100K row range for L3 cache efficiency
strategy_notes: Iteration 1 likely regressed due to zone map metadata loading overhead and SIMD intrinsic complexity outweighing benefits, or interaction with existing parallelism. Iteration 2 focuses on isolated, simple optimizations: (1) Reorder predicates by selectivity to short-circuit 98% of rows on discount check early, (2) Tune morsel size within 50-150K range for cache locality without implementing new techniques. These minimize architectural changes and should avoid the bloat that hurt Iter 1.
notes: After iteration 2, evaluate: if performance improves >10%, consolidate gains. If still regressed/flat, recommend STOP (no actionable optimizations with 1 iteration budget remaining). Current GenDB is 29.3ms vs DuckDB 20.2ms (~45% gap); target is 16.5ms. Predicate reordering + morsel tuning estimated 10-20% gain → ~24-26ms (still gap to target, but demonstrates progress without risk of further regression).
